key_themes: CMS Medicare Advantage rate increases for 2027
2026 EPS headwinds from Star Ratings and margin pressure
Attractive valuation with moderate analyst upside
summary: Humana (HUM) sentiment has improved to mildly positive in mid-April 2026. The stock has rallied YTD (~19.5%) and recently surged above its 50-day moving average after CMS finalized a 2.48% Medicare Advantage rate increase for 2027, with some analysts highlighting buy opportunities, an attractive ~1.7% dividend, and a valuation gap.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/healthcare/articles/humana-surges-above-50-day-133059094.html)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HUM/) Earlier disappointing 2026 guidance (at least $9.00 adjusted EPS vs. ~$12 expectations due to ~$3.5B Star Ratings headwind) caused a sharp drop in February, contributing to a Hold consensus from ~20-26 analysts with average price targets of ~$210-238 (from current ~$205).[[3]](https://public.com/stocks/hum/forecast-price-target)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/humana-shares-fall-as-2026-earnings-guidance-disappoints-investors-4499144)[[5]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/HUM/forecast/) Recent operational wins like the Tuesday Health palliative care partnership add optimism, but investors remain focused on execution ahead of the April 29 Q1 earnings report.[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HUM/)
sources: 15+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, Investing.com), ~5 relevant X posts (primarily corporate/operational updates), Investor discussions (Reddit threads, StockTwits, Yahoo Finance community)
